Assembly: The assembly daCusEpit1.1 is based on 83x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Some inversions between the haplotypes were detected: 32.4 to 43 Mbp on Chromosome 2 and 36.6 to 54.56 Mbp on Chromosome 7. The region from the start to 6.2 Mbp on Chromosome 1 is highly repetitive and the order and orientation is uncertain.

Assembly: The assembly ddSalPent1.hap1.1 is based on 69x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome appears to be the product of an alo-octoploid event. In each haplotype there is evidence of the four ancestral chromosomes. A number of scaffolds are left unassigned in the assembly that are single copy in the assembly, they may constitute something like a B chromosome or simply misassembled repeat sequences. These scaffolds are SCAFFOLD_40, 58, 69, 78,106 and 191.

Assembly: The assembly daCusEpit1.1 is based on 83x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS.
